operating_ranges.evidence: RAC tubes with embedded but virtually free-radiating heating element - Maximum element temperature: 1,250°C (2,280°F)
The maximum continuous duty temperature is reduced to 1,000°C (1,830°F) furnace temperature in a hydrogen atmosphere
FIBROTHAL® SHELLS - A, B AND D: ≤700 mm (≤27.6 in) ± 3 mm ± 0.12 in, >700 mm (>27.6 in) ± 5 mm ± 0.20 in
FIBROTHAL® SHELLS - C: ≤700 mm (≤27.6 in) ± 5 mm ± 0.20 in
FIBROTHAL® SHELLS - R 'E' AND R 'F': ≤700 mm (≤27.6 in) ± 5 mm ± 0.20 in, >700 mm (>27.6 in) ± 10 mm ± 0.39 in

degradation_mechanisms.evidence: Care is necessary when integrating heating elements due to the potential for corrosion

degradation_mechanisms.driven_by: linear shrinkage at maximum continuous duty temperature
degradation_mechanisms.evidence: Linear shrinkage, % (24 hours at max. continuous duty temperature) 3 /<1 to 4.5
iron oxide scale can damage Kanthal® heating elements, use a protective cover

fault_conditions.name: electrical_bridge_formation
fault_conditions.description: Formation of electrical bridges from salt or oxide condensates
fault_conditions.evidence: Vapours must not form condensates from salts or oxides, otherwise electrical bridges will be formed

fault_conditions.name: module_gap_formation
fault_conditions.description: Gaps between modules due to shrinkage causing radiant losses
fault_conditions.evidence: to prevent radiant losses through module gaps, we recommend placing a double-folded layer of ceramic fiber felt between Fibrothal® modules

maintenance_actions.name: joint_sealing
maintenance_actions.what_it_resets: prevents radiant losses through module gaps
maintenance_actions.evidence: place a double-folded layer of ceramic fiber felt between Fibrothal® modules. The ceramic fiber felt should extend at least 25 mm (0.98 in) beyond the front of the module

maintenance_actions.name: module_repair
maintenance_actions.evidence: CEMENT - Used to repair damaged Fibrothal® modules. REPAIR KIT - Includes adhesive, hardener, powder, wool, and felt

assumptions.statement: Shells are curved heating surfaces designed for cylindrical furnace applications
assumptions.reason: Component role provided and technical drawings show cylindrical shell with tapered ends and radius dimensions

assumptions.statement: Maximum operating temperature for shells is same as RAC tubes since both use free-radiating elements
assumptions.reason: RAC tubes are described as having embedded but virtually free-radiating heating elements with 1,250°C maximum, and shells would use similar heating element configuration
